what_it_does: Declares a stack item type named verify-request that represents work the fabric sends to an operator for human verification of a result or claim; it specifies an id, label, description, a row field mapping, an unsettled state, and notes that clearing happens via a verdict rather than a plain acknowledgement.
observations: The docstring frames an operator-cycle: fabric works -> verify-request is stacked -> a verdict is given -> work resumes.
The item clears on a VERDICT, not a plain acknowledge, distinguishing it from presentation/explanation items.
A comment notes that a verify surface (verify:open) and verdict mechanism are planned later, with a generic fallback for now.
The file contains no executable logic or code symbols; it is a data declaration.
standouts: Cleared by a verdict, not a simple acknowledgement.
Explicitly part of a human operator-cycle vocabulary.
Declares future verify:open/verdict mechanism that is not yet implemented.
conventions: Includes a path-prefixed module docstring explaining semantics.
References another entity via a dotted path (identity.headline) for row field data.
Uses a simple string state value (pending) for unsettled_state.
concerns: The verdict clearing mechanism and renderer are described as future work, so this file is only a partial declaration.
The open_verb is a generic fallback rather than a concrete implementation.
The identity.headline reference has no local schema or validation.
No symbols are defined; the file is purely declarative.
notes: A placeholder declaration for a human-verification stack item that will later need a verdict-driven clearing UI.
questions: What is the exact structure and allowed values of a verdict?
How does identity.headline resolve at runtime?
What will the verify:open surface and verdict mechanism look like?
How is this type ordered or prioritized relative to other stack item types?
Who or what is 'Tim' in the operator-cycle?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to declare the verify-request stack item type for operator verification, and the file does exactly that as a declarative specification.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to complete the vocabulary for a full operator-cycle by declaring the verify-request type now, even though its rendering surface and verdict-based clearing logic will be added later.
novelty: It explicitly distinguishes clearing semantics (verdict vs. acknowledgement) for a human verification step, which is a semantic nuance not common in generic task types.
contribution: Contributes the verify-request stack item type definition, including its label, description, row-field mapping, pending unsettled state, and verdict-based clearing semantics, so a fused system can represent and route human-verification work items.
summary_for_embedding: A declarative Python file that registers a verify-request stack item type for operator verification, specifying its metadata, pending state, and verdict-based clearing semantics.
intention_for_embedding: This file was written to add the verify-request type to the operator-cycle vocabulary ahead of implementing its renderer and verdict mechanism.
